_readme: The web-editable slice of serverDZ.cfg. Apply-ServerCfg.ps1 renders serverDZ.cfg at EVERY prestart from serverDZ.cfg.template + host.env (the two passwords) + this file, so a value changed in the web editor lands on the next restart with no deploy. ONLY the keys below may be set - the renderer's allowlist is closed, so anything else here is ignored with a warning. Everything NOT listed (password, passwordAdmin, verifySignatures, shardId, instanceId, guaranteedUpdates, serverTime, storageAutoFix, vppDisablePassword, adminLogPlayerList, loginQueue*, class Missions) stays owned by the template because changing it breaks server identity, persistence, or the boot itself: instanceId/shardId select the storage folder (a change reads as a full wipe), verifySignatures is a security control, and the active map comes from map.env, not the Missions class. Values are validated by type and range; a bad one is skipped with a warning and the template default stands. serverTimeAcceleration/serverNightTimeAcceleration drive the day/night cycle - see the Server tab's cycle sliders, or docs/SETUP.md for the math.
